mission.title: harbor: Proxy cache should serve cached images even if remote image is deleted
mission.description: Proxy cache should serve cached images even if remote image is deleted. Requested by 25+ users.

steps.title: Check current harbor deployment
steps.description:
Verify your harbor version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n harbor -l app.kubernetes.io/name=harbor
helm list -n harbor 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working harbor installation.

steps.title: Review harbor configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant harbor configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n harbor -l app.kubernetes.io/name=harbor
kubectl get configmap -n harbor -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=harbor
```
Currently, when using Harbor's proxy cache, if an image exists in the local cache but has been deleted from the remote repository, Harbor will not deliver the cached image.

steps.title: Upgrade Harbor to include the proxy cache fix
steps.description:
Upgrade Harbor to a version that includes the proxy cache improvement:
```bash
# Update the Harbor Helm repo
helm repo update harbor

# Upgrade Harbor
helm upgrade harbor harbor/harbor -n harbor -f values.yaml

# Verify pods restart successfully
kubectl rollout status deployment/harbor-core -n harbor
```
After upgrading, cached images will be served even if the remote image is deleted.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n harbor -l app.kubernetes.io/name=harbor
kubectl get events -n harbor --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Proxy cache should serve cached images even if remote image…" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: The proxy cache was updated to serve cached images even when the remote registry reports the image as deleted. Previously, if an image was deleted from the upstream registry, Harbor's proxy cache would return a 404 instead of serving the locally cached copy. The fix modifies the proxy cache logic to fall back to the local cache when the upstream returns a not-found error, ensuring cached images remain available.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with harbor installed or the issue environment reproducible.
